Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_TimeMode_tooltip: Defines at what time of day sprinters can use their special scream. 0 = day and night; 1 = day only; 2 = night only. Example: use '2' for calmer days and much more tense nights.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_ScreechChance_tooltip: Probability that a sprinter will let out a discovery scream the first time they see you. 0 = never screams; 100 = almost every sprinter screams as soon as they spot you. Example: 60 means roughly 6 out of 10 encounters start with a scream.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_ChaseScreechChance_too: Extra chance for a scream while the sprinter is already chasing you. Use low values (5–20) for occasional screams and higher ones for chaotic, noisy pursuits. Combine this with the cooldown setting to avoid endless screaming.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_ChaseCooldownHours_too: Minimum in-game hours between screams from the SAME sprinter during a chase. 0.01 ≈ about 36 in-game seconds. Higher values make chases quieter; lower ones make them louder and more frantic.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_GlobalVolume_tooltip: General volume multiplier applied to all screams in the mod. 1.0 = default; 0.5 = half volume; 2.0 = double volume (careful with your ears). Use this to balance the mod’s audio with the rest of the game.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_FarVolume_tooltip: Controls the volume of the discovery scream when the sprinter is still far away. Example: 0.1 gives a faint echo; values near 1.0 make the scream stand out even from a distance.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_NearVolume_tooltip: Volume multiplier when the sprinter screams right in your face. For a stronger jump scare, use 1.0 or higher; high values make screams much more aggressive.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_FarRange_tooltip: Maximum distance in tiles at which the discovery scream can be heard in 3D. Higher values make the sound travel farther, warning you of sprinters before you see them. Useful in open maps where echoes enhance immersion.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_AlertNearbyZombies_too: If enabled, the scream uses the game’s sound system to alert zombies within a set radius. If disabled, the scream is only aesthetic — it scares the player but doesn’t affect horde AI. Great for immersive sound without changing difficulty.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_AlertRadius_tooltip: The size of the area, in tiles, where zombies will hear the scream and walk toward the source. Example: 20 affects just the nearby street; 80–100 can wake up half the neighborhood. Very high values make every scream a mini-horde event.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_ClusterRadius_tooltip: Defines the size of the area considered a single screaming scene. If multiple sprinters are within this radius, they count as one group (cluster). The scream limit per group applies to all of them, preventing endless chaos in crowded areas.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_ClusterCooldownHours_t: Duration of the time window controlling screams per group (cluster), in in-game hours. Within this window, the total number of screams is limited by 'Max screams per group'. Example: 0.01 ≈ about 36 in-game seconds for that area.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_MaxClusterScreams_tool: Maximum number of screams allowed in each group during the set window. Example: with value 2, even if 10 sprinters spot you at once, only 2 screams will be heard in that scene.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_MaxProcessDistance_too: Maximum distance in tiles from the player within which theySEE YOU still processes sprinters for screams. Zombies beyond this radius are ignored to save performance. Values between 180 and 250 maintain immersion while staying efficient.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_TickRate_tooltip: How many ticks the game waits before reprocessing each sprinter. Lower values = faster reaction but more CPU load; higher = lighter but slower. 6 is a recommended balance for large hordes and helicopter events.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_EnableNearScream_toolt: When enabled, the sprinter emits an extra scream when entering close range (face scream), in addition to the discovery and chase screams. Disabling it reduces audio chaos in melee combat and keeps the mod cleaner in dense hordes.
Sandbox_SIMBA_TSY_NearAsWorldSound_toolt: If enabled, the close scream uses PlayWorldSound just like the distant one — full 3D sound with spatial direction and echo, perfect for headsets. If disabled, it uses the zombie’s local audio, sharper and more direct.
